rulings.Case Title: KOKO FUJITA FLOWERS VS. DAVID SEAN GILLIMORE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Extend Time For Completion Of Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 27, 2012, Line 4, PLAINTIFF KOKO FLOWERS' Motion To Extend Time For Completion Of Arbitration is Granted. The strong policy of deciding cases on their merits and the personal difficulties experienced by plaintiff's counsel outweighs the claimed prejudice suffered by defendants. However, plaintiff - either through her current counsel or through new counsel - must not delay any further. The arbitraton proceedings need to be initiated by April 13, 2012 and, unless delayed for reasons not attributable to plaintiff or her counsel, completed by April 13, 2013. Plaintiff must assume that there will not be any further extensions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 27, 2012, Line 7, PLAINTIFF CACH, LLC'S Hearing On Claim Of Exemption is Denied. Half of the money held, $507.45 is to be released to the judgment creditor and the other half, $507.46, is to be released to the judgment debtor. The judgment debtor has not provided any evidence tracing the funds to an exempt source. However, judgment debtor's financial statement shows that at least some of the money is needed to meet her basic living needs. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TIFFANY RUBY VS. STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY, AN ILLINOIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Pltfs' Form Interrogatrories (Set One) And Request For Monetary Sanctions Of $2670.23
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 27, 2012, Line 2. PLAINTIFF TIFFANY RUBY'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Form Interrogatrories (Set One) And Request For Monetary Sanctions Of $2670.23. Pro Tem Judge Trent Thornley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN B. LEWIS MD VS. DAVID MILLSTEIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 27, 2012, Line 17, DEFENDANTS DAVID MILLSTEIN, MILLSTEIN & ASSOCIATES DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled. Evidence Code 1119 is not a complete bar to any of plaintiff's claims. Because plaintiff's claims are based at least in part on the theory that he has been required to defend against a statute of limitations defense rather than that the defense bars his lawsuit against Jax Kneppers Associates, plaintiff is not judicially estopped from pursuing his claims. Plaintiff adequately alleges damages. If plaintiff desires a stay of this action, he needs to file a motion seeking such relief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JEFF DORIA VS. CLASS ACTION SERVICES, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 27, 2012, Line 21, DEFENDANT CLASS ACTION SERVICES, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Overruled. The declaratory relief action is adequately pled. The issue of whether the 2007 Agreement was a novation to the 2003 Agreement is a question of fact not amenable to resolution on a demurrer. The complaint does not affirmatively show that it is time-barred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CYPRESS SECURITY, LLC. A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Strike Portions; Req For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 27, 2012, Line 22, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Strike Portions of the Complaint; Request For Judicial Notice - Granted as to the amount of damages with ten days leave to amend to seek damages only for work performed in August 2010 and later and denied as to the request for attorney's fees. Per Government Code 911.2 and Stafford v. Oil Tool Corporation (1955) 133 Cal.App.2d 763, 766, all claims for services prior to August 2010 are time-barred. Because section 12 is reasonably susceptible to the interpretation that it is the functional equivalent of an attorney's fees clause, whether plaintiff may seek an award of fees is not amenable for resolution on this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: INS COMM STATE OF CA VS WESTERN EMPLOYER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Approval Of Commutation
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 27, 2012, Line 23, PETITIONER INSURANCE COMMISSIONER OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion For Approval Of Commutation is Granted. Good cause shown and no opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: INS COMM STATE OF CA VS WESTERN EMPLOYER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Approving The Policyholder Claims Approval Agreement With Manor Care, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 27, 2012, Line 23, PETITIONER INSURANCE COMMISSIONER OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion For An Order Approving The Policyholder Claims Approval Agreement With Manor Care, Inc. is Granted. Good cause shown and no opposition filed. Although not a basis to deny the motion, the court notes that the settlement sought to be approved was agreed to more than a year and a half ago and thus the parties have not acted with expected diligence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. =(302/HEK)
